title: Yashigani MCP OPA Input Schema
description: Input document schema for the mcp.rego policy package. Describes the structure of input passed by the gateway when evaluating MCP-posture requests and their responses.

posture.description: MCP deployment posture. mcp-a: local stdio Shape A, mcp-b: remote Streamable-HTTP Shape B, mcp-c: multi-hop chained Shape C.

action.description: The MCP action being requested. Use dot-notation: mcp.prompts.list, mcp.tools.call, mcp.resources.read, etc.

identity.description: Resolved caller identity, populated by the gateway from the SPIFFE certificate or JWT.

spiffe.description: SPIFFE URI of the immediate caller, e.g. spiffe://cluster.local/ns/default/sa/langflow. Empty string when SPIFFE is not present (treated as anonymous in policy).

chain.description: Full upstream identity chain for multi-hop MCP-C calls. Each entry is a SPIFFE URI representing a hop in call-order (index 0 = originating caller, last = immediate caller). Populated by the MCP-C JWT (P2/N3 chunk). Absent on mcp-a and mcp-b. Policy enforces max depth via mcp_chain_max_depth.

items.description: SPIFFE URI of a hop in the chain.

tool.description: Present when action is mcp.tools.call. Mutually exclusive with prompt and resource (oneOf enforced by policy). Must NOT be present together with prompt or resource.

name.description: Tool name as declared in the MCP server manifest's tools list.

args_redacted.description: Tool call arguments with all secret-like values redacted to '<REDACTED>'. The gateway must perform redaction before populating this field. The policy uses this field for audit capture only — never for allow/deny decisions on argument values.

prompt.description: Present when action is mcp.prompts.list or mcp.prompts.get. Mutually exclusive with tool and resource (oneOf enforced by policy).

name.description: Prompt name as declared in the MCP server manifest.

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity label of the prompt content. Absent when not classified — policy treats absent as PUBLIC (conservative for prompt listing, caller-ceiling-checked for prompt content).

resource.description: Present when action is mcp.resources.read or mcp.resources.list. Mutually exclusive with tool and prompt (oneOf enforced by policy).

uri.description: Resource URI as declared in the MCP server manifest, e.g. file:///data/reports or https://internal.example.com/docs/api.

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity label of the resource content. Absent when not classified — policy treats absent as PUBLIC.

agent.description: Agent context. Present when the caller is a registered Yashigani agent.

runtime.description: Agent runtime identifier, e.g. langflow, n8n, openai-assistant, custom. Used by policy for posture-specific enforcement.

budget.description: Budget context for cost-aware gating. All fields optional — absent budget fields are treated as unconstrained (allow). Operators who configure budgets populate this field via the gateway budget enforcer.

this_call_estimate.description: Estimated cost of this call in abstract token-equivalent units. null means unknown/unconstrained. Policy uses this for rate_limit_key selection but does NOT block on cost (budget blocking is handled by the gateway budget enforcer upstream of OPA).

allOf.description: tool, prompt, resource are mutually exclusive. Exactly zero or one may be present. (At least one SHOULD be present for tool/prompt/resource actions, but the policy does not require it at the schema level — absence of all three results in deny.)

$comment: Decision return shape (not validated here — returned by OPA, not sent to OPA): { allow: bool, redact_args: [string], audit_capture: bool, rate_limit_key: string | null }

mcp_decision.title: MCP OPA Decision Shape
mcp_decision.description: Shape returned by OPA at /v1/data/yashigani/mcp/mcp_decision. NOT an input field — documented here for gateway implementers.

deny_reason.description: Human-readable reason for the deny decision. Value is 'ok' when allow is true. Values: ok, missing_spiffe_identity, invalid_posture, chain_depth_exceeded, multiple_subjects_in_request, missing_subject, unrecognised_action, tool_not_in_exposed_allowlist, mcp_c_requires_chain.

redact_args.description: List of tool argument keys that must be redacted before logging. Empty when tool is not present.

audit_capture.description: Whether the gateway must write a full audit record (including redacted args) for this call.

rate_limit_key.description: Rate-limit bucket key for this call, e.g. spiffe + action + tool name. null means no rate limiting required.
